evidence: "Great docudrama of the east coast mob...also good to watch in conjunction with AMC's Beginnings of the Mob"
"minimal plot but lots of colorful scenes"
"the villainous banker who coerces the father to give him his daughter to save him from financial ruin..."
"the villain gets his..All that was missing was a saw mill or a girl tied to railroad tracks."
"Well produced but it is the 'same old, same old' story."
"the two strip colors, of course are crisp and the film itself is spotlessly cleaned."
"the print is only fair, but this is film that has been in the 'cheap dvd' library for years."
"One of Busby Berkeley's most controversial films."
"I am a fan of these old 'revue' movies from the early age of Talkies."
"the brilliance is exhibited by a fashion show (ala The Women)."
"the camera focuses on each of the girl's faces."
"If you remember being a kid like me... you'll get a kick out of remembering these old adventures..."

summary: The user has a strong preference for nostalgic content, particularly from early Hollywood and musicals. They appreciate high production quality and emotional resonance, often reflecting on memories associated with the films they watch.
